parts.answer: Rises from origin and curves with decreasing gradient Finishes horizontal B1 B1

Speed is scalar Velocity is vector Speed has magnitude / size / value (only) Velocity has magnitude / size / value and direction OR velocity has direction; speed does not B1 B1 Total: Page 5 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 41 (P =) hdg OR 15 × × 1.5 × or 150 Pa or N / m or 150 kPa or kN / m C1 A1 (F =) PA OR 150 000 × 9.0 x 10 N / 9.0 × kN C1 A1 Same value as (a)(ii) or 9.0 × N B1

Weight of tanker has to be equal to upward force of water Depth (below surface) is / becomes less OR Tanker rises (Tanker rises) because pressure / force on bottom of tanker is greater OR because upthrust greater OR At same depth as in river, pressure / force on bottom of tanker is higher so tanker rises B1 M1 A1 Total: / they) collide with / hit walls of container OR rebound from walls of container Change of momentum OR Rate of change of momentum occurs OR F = (mv – mu) / t B1 B1 (760 + 120 =) 880 mmHg B1 Same value as (b)(i) or 880 mmHg B1 New pressure = (760 + 240 =) 1000 (mmHg) PV = constant OR P V = P V OR 12 × 880 = V × cm C1 C1 A1 Total: Page 6 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 41 Molecules with higher / highest (kinetic) energy OR that gain enough energy escape (from the liquid surface) Molecules remaining in liquid have low / lower (kinetic) energy OR Energy for evaporation came from remaining liquid B1 B1 B1 B1

questions.question: 4 (a) In an experiment, cold water is poured into a bowl made of an insulating material. The container is placed in a draught-free room. After several hours, the volume and the temperature of the water are found to have decreased. Name and describe the process that causes the decrease in the volume of the water, and explain why the temperature of the water
questions.text: 4 (a) In an experiment, cold water is poured into a bowl made of an insulating material. The container is placed in a draught-free room. After several hours, the volume and the temperature of the water are found to have decreased. Name and describe the process that causes the decrease in the volume of the water, and explain why the temperature of the water

parts.answer: Greater decrease in temperature and / or volume than in (a). Fan removes vapour / blows vapour away / reduces humidity / reduces return of vapour to liquid, allowing more molecules to escape OR faster / more evaporation B1 B1

Metal is a good (thermal) conductor so passes heat to the liquid or from the surroundings (raising its temperature) B1 B1 Total: Page 7 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 41 Ice: in lattice / regular / arranged / orderly / fixed in place Water: random / irregular / not arranged / not orderly Molecular movement: Ice: vibrate Water: move (around) or slide over each other B2 d = m / V in any form OR (m =) Vd OR 1800 × 0.025 × = 41 000 kg C1 A1 (H =) mL OR 41 × 3.3 × 1.4 x 10 J OR 1.4 × kJ OR 1,4 × MJ C1 A1 Total Mark 300 – 360 m / s B1 20 Hz – 20 kHz B1 v = f λ OR (f =) v / λ OR (a)(i) / 0.022 Correct answer: e.g. 330 m / s gives 15 Hz C1 A1 Vertical dotted lines midway (by eye ) between each pair of compressions OR to right or left of compressions shown wit h correct spacing (by eye) B1 (At rarefactions) molecules have above normal separation / far apart / spread out Pressure (of air) is below normal / low OR Molecules exert below normal / low pressure B1 B1 Total : Page 8 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 41 Ray continues through first face, without bending, to sloping face Ray reflected vertically down at sloping face M1 A1 Prism drawn with correct orientation in square Correct reflection to produce emergent ray M1 A1
Tick in box 2 Tick in box 6 B1 B1 Total: B1

Chemical (energy) to el ectrical (energy) (in battery) Electrical (energy) to thermal / heat (energy) (in resistor) B1 B1 Total: Page 9 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 41 Mention of overheating or fire seen anywhere Mention of electric shock or electrocution seen anywhere Any two of: Fire / overheating: if thin / extension cable carries too large a current OR because thin / extension cable has no fuse. Fire / overheating due to extension cable being coiled (so that escape of heat is prevented) Electric shock / electrocution (of gardener) if unsuitable socket lets in moisture / gets wet Electric shock / electrocution (of gardener) if tape repair lets in moisture / gets wet Electric shock / electrocution if cable is cut by mower and no circuit-breaker B1 B1 B2 Total: Page 10 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper 0625 41
(Wire) moves vertically or down (page) Moves up (page) OR Magnetic field is into the page OR (Fleming’s) left hand-rule applies C1 A1 B1
